releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Maintenance release for the Obsidian community-directory review.

## Changes
- Removed the disallowed `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` eslint-disables by moving the settings-tab placeholders (`Calendar/Days`, `YYYY-MM-DD`) into the i18n message table. No user-facing behavior change.

See **1.0.0** for the full feature overview.

## Install (manual)
Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/monthly-tracker/`, then enable the plugin in **Settings → Community plugins**. Requires Obsidian v1.8.7+.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
First release of **Monthly Tracker** — visualize your daily-note data as monthly color-coded strips, right inside your notes. Turn habits, moods, exercise, sleep, and anything else you log into patterns you can read at a glance.

## Features
- **Boolean** — highlight days where a property is truthy (habit/completion tracking). Omit the property to track by file existence (journals, logs).
- **Colormap** — map string values to colors to classify by label (mood, workout type, focus level, …).
- **Heatmap** — shade days by numeric value (sleep hours, distance, pages), with built-in color schemes, custom colors, units, and an optional monthly total.
- Click any day cell to open that note; hover for Obsidian's page preview.
- Auto-detects your daily-notes folder (Daily Notes / Periodic Notes), configurable date format, and English/Korean UI.

## Quick start
In a monthly note whose frontmatter has `year` and `month`:

~~~markdown
```monthly-tracker
type: boolean
property: workout
color: blue
```
~~~

See the [README](https://github.com/younginshin115/obsidian-monthly-tracker#readme) for all options and examples ([한국어](https://github.com/younginshin115/obsidian-monthly-tracker/blob/main/README.ko.md)).

## Install (manual)
Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/monthly-tracker/`, then enable the plugin in **Settings → Community plugins**.

**Requires** Obsidian v1.8.7+. Works on desktop and mobile.
